title: Medical Necessity Criteria for Bariatric Surgery

rules.rule_text: Procedures are considered medically necessary when ALL of the following criteria are met
Informed consent with appropriate explanation of risks, benefits, and alternatives
Adult aged 18 years or older with documentation of
Body mass index (BMI) ≥40
BMI greater ≥35 with ONE of the following severe obesity-related comorbidities
Clinically significant cardio-pulmonary disease (e.g. severe obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), obesity-hypoventilation syndrome (OHS))
Coronary artery disease, objectively documented via stress test, echocardiography, angiography, prior myocardial infarction, or similar
Objectively documented cardiomyopathy
Medically refractory hypertension (defined as > 140 mmHg systolic and/or 90 mmHg diastolic despite concurrent use of 3 antihypertensive agents)
Type 2 diabetes mellitus
Nonalcoholic fatty liver disease or nonalcoholic steatohepatitis
Osteoarthritis of the knee or hip or improving outcomes of knees or hip replacement
Urinary stress incontinence
BMI ≥30-34.9, see section below
Failure to achieve and maintain successful long-term weight loss via non-surgical therapy
The proposed bariatric surgery includes a comprehensive pre- and post-operative plan
Preoperative evaluation to rule out and treat any other reversible causes of weight gain/obesity
Basic laboratory testing (blood glucose, lipid panel, CBC, metabolic panel, blood typing, coagulation studies)
Nutrient deficiency screening and formal nutrition evaluation
Cardiopulmonary risk evaluation
GI evaluation
Endocrine evaluation
Age appropriate cancer screening verified complete and up to date
Smoking cessation counseling, if applicable
Psycho-social behavioral evaluation
No current substance abuse has been identified
Members who have any of the following conditions MUST have formal, documented preoperative psychological clearance
A history of schizophrenia, borderline personality disorder, suicidal ideation, severe depression
Who are currently under the care of a psychologist/psychiatrist
Who are on psychotropic medications
